reasoning: Ouattara has a strong track record of delaying his official presidential candidacy announcements to minimize internal party friction and maximize political leverage (as seen in the 2020 election when he announced in August for an October election). While he has strongly hinted at a run—such as his January 2025 statement expressing a desire to continue serving—he explicitly stated he would consider it if his party backed him. This shifts the focus to the ruling RHDP's timeline. The RHDP is scheduled to hold a party congress on June 21-22, 2025. It is highly likely that Ouattara will use that congress, or a date subsequent to it, as the platform for a formal declaration or acceptance of the party's nomination. Announcing before June 1, absent a major precipitating event, would preempt the planned political timeline and his established pattern of 'shillyshallying.' The probability of a formal declaration before this June 1 cutoff is therefore relatively low.
batch_queries: Will Alassane Ouattara declare his candidacy for president of Ivory Coast (Côte d’Ivoire) before June 1, 2025?
